content: When a drug recall occurs, healthcare providers must: (1) Identify affected lots using recall notices on FDA website; (2) Quarantine inventory immediately; (3) Notify patients who received the product, prioritizing Class I recalls; (4) Arrange replacement therapy with alternative products; (5) Document all actions in patient records; (6) Report adverse events related to recalled products to FDA MedWatch. For Class I recalls involving critical medications (e.g., sterile injectables, life-sustaining drugs), urgent patient notification within 24-48 hours is recommended. Pharmacies must maintain recall logs and participate in FDA effectiveness checks.
